summary: Add a dedicated, failure-retaining M4 browser-hosted Playwright journey that exercises real WASM rendering, memory cache reuse, geometry bounds deltas, reload-persisted thumbnails, serialized animation, and the exact sanitized seven-response proposal/agent/cap sequence while explicitly keeping MCP stdio evidence packaged-desktop-only.

testEvidence.criterion: The repository exposes a dedicated, serial, artifact-retaining M4 hosted Playwright command and CI lane without absorbing packaged-desktop MCP claims.
testEvidence.test: Run the command before wiring, add the dedicated test/config/package/CI paths, then validate the scoped source, TypeScript graph, and CI YAML structure.

red.outcome: RED: pnpm exited 1 because test:e2e:m4-hosted did not exist.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec biome check tests/e2e/m4-hosted-journey.e2e.ts tests/e2e/m4-hosted.playwright.config.ts package.json; pnpm.cmd exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false; parse .github/workflows/ci.yml with the pinned yaml dependency
green.outcome: GREEN for static wiring only: Biome checked 3 scoped files with no fixes, strict TypeScript exited zero, and ci.yml parsed with 12 e2e steps. Browser runtime is explicitly not called green by this observation.

testEvidence.criterion: Hosted evidence retains startup and failure diagnostics, the local provider shuts down within a forced bound, and the dedicated journey stays isolated from the default and production-static lanes.
testEvidence.test: Run only the M4 hosted runner, M4 packaged-walkthrough helper, and production-static browser source-contract unit files after the independent review findings are implemented.

red.outcome: RED: 1 of 3 source-contract tests failed because the default Playwright ignore assertion still expected the old two-file one-line list and did not account for the dedicated M4 hosted journey. Independent review also found no retained wrapper log before Playwright startup, unbounded mock shutdown, and incomplete agent, thumbnail-reload, and animation assertions.

green.outcome: GREEN: 3 focused files and 14 tests passed. The wrapper retained success, exact-status failure, thrown-spawn, null-status, and direct-to-file output larger than Node's default synchronous child buffer; the mock forced a connected, server-observed held request closed within its configured bound and stayed concurrent/idempotent; and the structural source contract recognized the exact three-test default ignore list and dedicated artifact runner. The first forced-close assertion correctly failed when it inspected the client socket before the close event arrived; the final test awaits that bounded event and proves the socket was destroyed. This is unit/source proof only, not a hosted browser runtime pass.

nearMisses.sought: Whether the production-only AC-15.a paint benchmark belonged in the new development-server journey
nearMisses.confirmation: The focused run measured 334.400 ms under Vite development while the existing production-static evidence previously measured 37.7 ms. The benchmark remains owned by the optimized production-static lane and the hosted journey checks only a cache hit with zero additional engine runs.

nearMisses.sought: Which geometry delta is truthfully available from browser WASM
nearMisses.confirmation: The real hosted run reported volume unavailable and the exact bounds-size delta +2/0/0 mm. The hosted assertion follows that observable contract; the existing packaged-native walkthrough remains the signed-volume authority.

nearMisses.sought: Whether CI would retain actionable evidence when Playwright fails before reporter initialization
nearMisses.confirmation: The first CI wiring depended only on Playwright's artifact directory. The replacement runner pre-creates the directory, replaces stale logs, captures stdout/stderr plus exact command status, and CI additionally tees its launcher output before the always-upload step.

nearMisses.sought: Whether same-session labels could overstate agent, thumbnail, or animation coverage
nearMisses.confirmation: Independent review found that one proposal request, same-session images, and immediate Play/Pause labels were insufficient. The journey now requires the exact seven-response tool sequence with review approval and cap, reload/reopen byte-identical 240 by 160 thumbnails, exactly one completed scrub render, one new serialized Play render that advances from frame 51 to frame 52 after the current-frame cache hit, no overlap, and stable paused state.

nearMisses.sought: Whether a held local-provider request could hang teardown or make a failed close look complete
nearMisses.confirmation: The prior helper marked itself closed before server.close succeeded and had no deadline. Cleanup now shares one in-flight promise, marks closed only after success, closes idle connections, forcibly closes active connections after a bounded grace, and retains retry semantics on failure. The focused regression waits for TCP connect and server receipt of a partial request, proves shutdown remains pending before the grace, then awaits the actual client close event and confirms socket destruction.

nearMisses.sought: The first bounded post-remediation hosted-browser runtime result
nearMisses.confirmation: The runner retained exit status 1 and a trace after the journey passed real geometry, cache, reload-thumbnail, and animation checks, then Playwright correctly rejected the non-exact AI model label because it also matched Configured AI models. Both uses now require the exact AI model label.

nearMisses.sought: The second and final local hosted-browser recheck result
nearMisses.confirmation: The recheck passed the exact settings selector and reached accepted AI proposal application. It then retained exit status 1 because the test expected the visible CodeMirror source without the canonical trailing newline produced by fenced-code proposal extraction. The editor expectation now includes that one newline; no third local browser run was started.

questions: The hosted browser journey remains unverified, not green. After the original three-iteration development cap, two explicitly bounded post-review executions each stopped on a distinct test-only expectation and retained complete evidence. The exact-label and canonical-newline corrections await the ordinary exact-head hosted CI execution; no third local browser run is authorized.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
